revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Ootsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine front view |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}} Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}} {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—{{ill|Geungusu of Baekje|ja|近仇首王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geungusu of Baekje|qq=}}’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}, the shrine became a religious corporation under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}} in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated {{ill|Cultural property|ja|文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural property|qq=}} of {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}} {{ill|Takawashi Station|ja|高鷲駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takawashi Station|qq=}} - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{translated page|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:Pages with erroneous histories]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]]

Shrine front view |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}} Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}} {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—{{ill|Geungusu of Baekje|ja|近仇首王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geungusu of Baekje|qq=}}’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}, the shrine became a religious corporation under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}} in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated {{ill|Cultural property|ja|文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural property|qq=}} of {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}} {{ill|Takawashi Station|ja|高鷲駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takawashi Station|qq=}} - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:Pages with erroneous histories]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]]

It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}} {{ill|Takawashi Station|ja|高鷲駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takawashi Station|qq=}} - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:pages needing history import]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]]

revision.comment: 26 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市) up to rev 99966973
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大津神社 |画像 = [[File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]高鷲8丁目1-2 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 11.34 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 34 | 経度秒 = 58.05 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]
[[奇稻田姫命]]
[[天日鷲神|天日鷲命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 伝・6世紀 |本殿 = |別名 = 丹下（たんげ）の大社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 秋の例大祭（10月7･8日） |神事 = }} '''大津神社'''（おおつじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[奇稻田姫命]]、[[天日鷲神|天日鷲命]] * 配祀神 - [[大山咋命]]、[[菅原道真]] == 歴史 == 当社の創建年代は6世紀ともいわれているが、当社は10世紀初めに[[醍醐天皇]]の御代に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]の[[河内国]][[丹比郡]]に「大津神社三座鍬・靱」と明記されている由緒の古い神社であり「丹下の郷の大宮」と称せられていた[https://www.otsujinja.net/ 大津神社ホームページ 御由緒]。当時、この地方には、[[近仇首王]]（貴須王）の子孫といわれる[[葛井氏]]・[[船氏]]・[[津氏]]の三氏が勢力を張っており、この三氏のうち津氏一族がこの地をトして「大宮山」と称し、自分達の守護神を奉斎したことが当社の発祥だろうという古来からの定説がある。 当社は津氏一族の守護神として創祀されたようであるが、津氏一族が朝廷に召されて[[大和国]]に移住し、また時代の推移に伴って[[氏姓制度]]が衰退していくと、中世以降には当社はこの地方九ヶ村の人々の氏神として受け継がれ、「河内の大宮」と称せられた。その後、仏教の隆盛に伴い[[本地垂迹説]]に基づいて「[[牛頭天王]]社」と称し、境内に[[真言宗]]の宮寺である大宮山南之坊を設けて[[神仏混淆]]となり、社僧の支配を受けることとなる。こうして広く世の人々からは「北宮の牛頭さん」と称せられた。 [[明治]]時代になると新しい神社制度が定められ、[[神仏習合]]が厳しく禁じられて南之坊は廃寺とされ、当社は高鷲・埴生両村の氏神として[[1872年]]（明治5年）5月に[[村社]]に列格され、大津神社と改称された。 [[太平洋戦争]]後は、[[連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|GHQ]]による占領政策である[[神道指令]]によって国家の管理を離れ、[[1946年]]（[[昭和]]21年）6月2日、[[宗教法人]]令に基づき[[神社本庁]]所属の宗教法人としての大津神社が発足し現在に至る。 今日では、[[羽曳野市]]西部、南西部、広域の氏神（産土神、鎮守神）として崇敬されている。 厄除開運、縁結び、安産、家内安全、交通安全、引越しや住居のリフォーム等の方災除けのご利益があるとされる。おついたち（一日）は一日参りで賑わうが、平素は人通りが少なく、静かである。羽曳野市内の氏神様として認識されている。 == 境内 == * 本殿（羽曳野市指定有形[[文化財]]） - [[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）再建[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/bukkaku/2445.html 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]。 * 幣殿 - 寛永17年（1640年）再建。 * 拝殿 - 寛永17年（1640年）再建。 * 社務所 === 摂末社 === * 大宮[[弁天|辯天]]宮 - 祭神：[[市杵嶋姫命]]。仏教建築である八角堂となっている。祭神は古くは北宮の宮池の小さな浮島に祀られていたもの。 * 大宮戎社 - 祭神：[[えびす|戎大神]]。[[1987年]]（昭和62年）12月19日建立。 * 大宮[[稲荷]]社 - 祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]。[[1972年]]（昭和47年）3月建立。 * 平和之社（やわらぎのやしろ） - 祭神：平和之神（氏子であった戦没英霊278柱）。[[サンフランシスコ講和条約]]締結を記念して[[1951年]]（昭和26年）に創建された。 ** 本殿 - [[1975年]]（昭和51年）に新たに建立。 ** 拝殿 - 1975年（昭和51年）に新たに建立。 == 文化財 == === 羽曳野市指定有形文化財 === * 本殿：附 棟札 == 祭事 == 当社では年に2回祭りが開催される。その都度、付近の道路に通行規制が敷かれる。秋は[[地車]]が曳航されるため、規制が増える。 * 夏祭り：毎年7月7、8日に開催される祭り。付近に祭りが少ないため、子供連れでごった返す。[[2022年]]（[[令和]]4年）からは夏祭りは屋台が出ない。 * 秋祭り（秋の例大祭）：毎年10月7、8日に開催される祭り。市内の[[向野 (羽曳野市)|向野地区]]から地車が曳航される。 == 所在地 == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * 大阪府羽曳野市高鷲8丁目1-2 == アクセス == * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄南大阪線|南大阪線]] [[高鷲駅]]より徒歩約3分 * 最寄バス停：[[近鉄バス松原営業所 | 近鉄バス]]高鷲駅前下車すぐ（土曜日の昼間1本のみ） * 最寄バス停：羽曳野市公共施設循環バス「う～すけ」高鷲駅前下車すぐ == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html 大津神社 - 大阪府神社庁] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] to [[Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 大津神社 |画像 = [[File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]高鷲8丁目1-2 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 11.34 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 34 | 経度秒 = 58.05 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]
[[奇稻田姫命]]
[[天日鷲神|天日鷲命]] |社格 = [[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 伝・6世紀 |本殿 = |別名 = 丹下（たんげ）の大社 |札所等 = |例祭 = 秋の例大祭（10月7･8日） |神事 = }} '''大津神社'''（おおつじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞嗚尊]]、[[奇稻田姫命]]、[[天日鷲神|天日鷲命]] * 配祀神 - [[大山咋命]]、[[菅原道真]] == 歴史 == 当社の創建年代は6世紀ともいわれているが、当社は10世紀初めに[[醍醐天皇]]の御代に編纂された[[延喜式神名帳]]の[[河内国]][[丹比郡]]に「大津神社三座鍬・靱」と明記されている由緒の古い神社であり「丹下の郷の大宮」と称せられていた[https://www.otsujinja.net/ 大津神社ホームページ 御由緒]。当時、この地方には、[[近仇首王]]（貴須王）の子孫といわれる[[葛井氏]]・[[船氏]]・[[津氏]]の三氏が勢力を張っており、この三氏のうち津氏一族がこの地をトして「大宮山」と称し、自分達の守護神を奉斎したことが当社の発祥だろうという古来からの定説がある。 当社は津氏一族の守護神として創祀されたようであるが、津氏一族が朝廷に召されて[[大和国]]に移住し、また時代の推移に伴って[[氏姓制度]]が衰退していくと、中世以降には当社はこの地方九ヶ村の人々の氏神として受け継がれ、「河内の大宮」と称せられた。その後、仏教の隆盛に伴い[[本地垂迹説]]に基づいて「[[牛頭天王]]社」と称し、境内に[[真言宗]]の宮寺である大宮山南之坊を設けて[[神仏混淆]]となり、社僧の支配を受けることとなる。こうして広く世の人々からは「北宮の牛頭さん」と称せられた。 [[明治]]時代になると新しい神社制度が定められ、[[神仏習合]]が厳しく禁じられて南之坊は廃寺とされ、当社は高鷲・埴生両村の氏神として[[1872年]]（明治5年）5月に[[村社]]に列格され、大津神社と改称された。 [[太平洋戦争]]後は、[[連合国軍最高司令官総司令部|GHQ]]による占領政策である[[神道指令]]によって国家の管理を離れ、[[1946年]]（[[昭和]]21年）6月2日、[[宗教法人]]令に基づき[[神社本庁]]所属の宗教法人としての大津神社が発足し現在に至る。 今日では、[[羽曳野市]]西部、南西部、広域の氏神（産土神、鎮守神）として崇敬されている。 厄除開運、縁結び、安産、家内安全、交通安全、引越しや住居のリフォーム等の方災除けのご利益があるとされる。おついたち（一日）は一日参りで賑わうが、平素は人通りが少なく、静かである。羽曳野市内の氏神様として認識されている。 == 境内 == * 本殿（羽曳野市指定有形[[文化財]]） - [[寛永]]17年（[[1640年]]）再建[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/soshiki/shougaigakushu/bunka-sekai/bunkazai/bunkazai/iseki_shokai/bukkaku/2445.html 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]。 * 幣殿 - 寛永17年（1640年）再建。 * 拝殿 - 寛永17年（1640年）再建。 * 社務所 === 摂末社 === * 大宮[[弁天|辯天]]宮 - 祭神：[[市杵嶋姫命]]。仏教建築である八角堂となっている。祭神は古くは北宮の宮池の小さな浮島に祀られていたもの。 * 大宮戎社 - 祭神：[[えびす|戎大神]]。[[1987年]]（昭和62年）12月19日建立。 * 大宮[[稲荷]]社 - 祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]。[[1972年]]（昭和47年）3月建立。 * 平和之社（やわらぎのやしろ） - 祭神：平和之神（氏子であった戦没英霊278柱）。[[サンフランシスコ講和条約]]締結を記念して[[1951年]]（昭和26年）に創建された。 ** 本殿 - [[1975年]]（昭和51年）に新たに建立。 ** 拝殿 - 1975年（昭和51年）に新たに建立。 == 文化財 == === 羽曳野市指定有形文化財 === * 本殿：附 棟札 == 祭事 == 当社では年に2回祭りが開催される。その都度、付近の道路に通行規制が敷かれる。秋は[[地車]]が曳航されるため、規制が増える。 * 夏祭り：毎年7月7、8日に開催される祭り。付近に祭りが少ないため、子供連れでごった返す。[[2022年]]（[[令和]]4年）からは夏祭りは屋台が出ない。 * 秋祭り（秋の例大祭）：毎年10月7、8日に開催される祭り。市内の[[向野 (羽曳野市)|向野地区]]から地車が曳航される。 == 所在地 == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * 大阪府羽曳野市高鷲8丁目1-2 == アクセス == * [[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄南大阪線|南大阪線]] [[高鷲駅]]より徒歩約3分 * 最寄バス停：[[近鉄バス松原営業所 | 近鉄バス]]高鷲駅前下車すぐ（土曜日の昼間1本のみ） * 最寄バス停：羽曳野市公共施設循環バス「う～すけ」高鷲駅前下車すぐ == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html 大津神社 - 大阪府神社庁] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおつしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

{{Shrine |name = Ootsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine front view |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}} Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}} {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—{{ill|Geungusu of Baekje|ja|近仇首王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geungusu of Baekje|qq=}}’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}, the shrine became a religious corporation under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}} in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated {{ill|Cultural property|ja|文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural property|qq=}} of {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}} {{ill|Takawashi Station|ja|高鷲駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takawashi Station|qq=}} - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:pages needing history import]] [[Category:Qq]][[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}} {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—{{ill|Geungusu of Baekje|ja|近仇首王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geungusu of Baekje|qq=}}’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}, the shrine became a religious corporation under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}} in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated {{ill|Cultural property|ja|文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural property|qq=}} of {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}} {{ill|Takawashi Station|ja|高鷲駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takawashi Station|qq=}} - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:pages needing history import]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}, the shrine became a religious corporation under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}} in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated {{ill|Cultural property|ja|文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural property|qq=}} of {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}} {{ill|Takawashi Station|ja|高鷲駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takawashi Station|qq=}} - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Shrine |name = Ootsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine front view |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}} Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}} {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—{{ill|Geungusu of Baekje|ja|近仇首王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geungusu of Baekje|qq=}}’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}, the shrine became a religious corporation under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}} in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated {{ill|Cultural property|ja|文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural property|qq=}} of {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}} {{ill|Takawashi Station|ja|高鷲駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takawashi Station|qq=}} - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Shrine |name = Ootsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine front view |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}} Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}} {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—{{ill|Geungusu of Baekje|ja|近仇首王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geungusu of Baekje|qq=}}’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}, the shrine became a religious corporation under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}} in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated {{ill|Cultural property|ja|文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural property|qq=}} of {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}} {{ill|Takawashi Station|ja|高鷲駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takawashi Station|qq=}} - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—{{ill|Geungusu of Baekje|ja|近仇首王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geungusu of Baekje|qq=}}’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}} {{ill|Takawashi Station|ja|高鷲駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takawashi Station|qq=}} - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the {{ill|Kawachi Province|ja|河内国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawachi Province|qq=}} {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—{{ill|Geungusu of Baekje|ja|近仇首王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Geungusu of Baekje|qq=}}’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国|qq=Yamato Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Province|qq=}} and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}, the shrine became a religious corporation under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}} in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}, offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated {{ill|Cultural property|ja|文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cultural property|qq=}} of {{ill|Habikino City|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino City|qq=}}) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}} {{ill|Takawashi Station|ja|高鷲駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takawashi Station|qq=}} - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Ootsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine front view |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]] Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]]
