The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:羽曳野市の建築物]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

Shrine front view |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]] Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[en:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]]

{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:羽曳野市の歴史]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}

It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino City]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Ootsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine front view |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]] Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:History of Habikino City]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}

{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Ōtsu Shrine errors]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}

Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}

{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}

Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}

Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}

In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}

{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}

Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11437361]] Wikidata name: Otsu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ootsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg|250px]]
Shrine front view |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]] Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|qq=}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|qq=}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[sn:Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)]] [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}}
