Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.5698167|N|135.5827917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神|1=Ōyamakui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935105}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|1=Tajihinokōri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368538}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約|1=Treaty of San Francisco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392541}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|1=Kintetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639080}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.5698167|N|135.5827917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神|1=Ōyamakui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935105}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|1=Tajihinokōri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368538}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約|1=Treaty of San Francisco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392541}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|1=Kintetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639080}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約|1=Treaty of San Francisco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392541}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|1=Kintetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639080}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11437361}}

{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.5698167|N|135.5827917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神|1=Ōyamakui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935105}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|1=Tajihinokōri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368538}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約|1=Treaty of San Francisco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392541}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|1=Kintetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639080}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino) (Q11437361)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Tajihinokōri district|ja|丹比郡|zh|丹比郡|WD=Q11368538}} || {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)|lt=Otsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|WD=Q11437361}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei}} ({{ill|Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver|WD=Q135009152|lt=Hoe and Quiver}}) || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || 4 seats || — || {{coord|34.569857|135.582761|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180963 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Inferior qid generated pages]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神|1=Ōyamakui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935105}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|1=Tajihinokōri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368538}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約|1=Treaty of San Francisco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392541}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|1=Kintetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639080}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino) (Q11437361)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. [[sn:commons:Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)]][[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11437361]] Wikidata name: Otsu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ootsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]] Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.5698167|N|135.5827917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神|1=Ōyamakui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935105}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|1=Tajihinokōri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368538}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約|1=Treaty of San Francisco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392541}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|1=Kintetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639080}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] {{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino) (Q11437361)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Tajihinokōri district|ja|丹比郡|zh|丹比郡|WD=Q11368538}} || {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)|lt=Otsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|WD=Q11437361}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] ({{ill|Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver|WD=Q135009152|lt=Hoe and Quiver}}) || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || 4 seats || — || {{coord|34.569857|135.582761|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180963 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]]

The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. [[sn:commons:Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)]][[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11437361]] Wikidata name: Otsu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ootsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]] Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.5698167|N|135.5827917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神|1=Ōyamakui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935105}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|1=Tajihinokōri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368538}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約|1=Treaty of San Francisco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392541}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|1=Kintetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639080}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino) (Q11437361)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Tajihinokōri district|ja|丹比郡|zh|丹比郡|WD=Q11368538}} || {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)|lt=Otsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|WD=Q11437361}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] ({{ill|Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver|WD=Q135009152|lt=Hoe and Quiver}}) || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || 4 seats || — || {{coord|34.569857|135.582761|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180963 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11437361}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]

In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|1=Kintetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639080}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino) (Q11437361)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. revision.comment: Text replacement - "is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō." to "is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]."
revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)]][[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11437361]] Wikidata name: Otsu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Ootsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka) haiden.jpg |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]] Takawashi 8-chome 1-2 | Latitude = 34 | Latitude Minutes = 34 | Latitude Seconds = 11.34 | N = N | Longitude = 135 | Longitude Minutes = 34 | Longitude Seconds = 58.05 | E = E | Map Country Code = JP | Map = Japan Osaka |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}
{{ill|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q505469}}
{{ill|Amaterasu's Messenger|ja|天日鷲命|lt=Amewakahiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu's Messenger|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Traditional: 6th century |Main Hall = |Other names = Tange no Taisha (丹下の大社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |Festivals = Autumn Grand Festival (October 7–8) |Rituals = | location = {{ill|Habikino|WD=Q694308}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.5698167|N|135.5827917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} }} '''Ootsu Shrine''' (大津神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: ** {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} ** {{ill|Kushinadahime|lt=Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto|ja|奇稻田姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushinadahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ}} ** {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja|天日鷲命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神|1=Amenohiwashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10939697}} * Secondary deities: ** {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|ja|大山咋命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamakui no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大山咋神|1=Ōyamakui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10935105}} ** {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == History == The shrine's founding is traditionally attributed to the 6th century. However, it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Engishiki Jinmyō-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} compiled during Emperor Daigo’s reign in the early 10th century as "Ootsu Shrine, three seats, Kuwari, and Yoroi" in the [[:en:Kawachi Province|Kawachi Province]] {{ill|Tanpi District|ja|丹比郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanpi District|1=Tajihinokōri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11368538}}. It has long been known as "The Grand Shrine of Tange no Sato."[https://www.otsujinja.net/ Ootsu Shrine Official Website - History]. In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Features an octagonal Buddhist-style hall.. * Ōmiya Ebisu Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ebisu|ja|戎大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約|1=Treaty of San Francisco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392541}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|1=Kintetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639080}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino) (Q11437361)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Tajihinokōri district|ja|丹比郡|zh|丹比郡|WD=Q11368538}} || {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino)|lt=Otsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)|WD=Q11437361}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] ({{ill|Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver|WD=Q135009152|lt=Hoe and Quiver}}) || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || 4 seats || — || {{coord|34.569857|135.582761|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=180963 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] {{wikidata link|Q11437361}}

In ancient times, this area was dominated by three clans—[[:en:Geungusu of Baekje|Geungusu of Baekje]]’s descendants: the Kazuraki, Funashi, and Tsu clans. The Tsu clan established the shrine in this area, naming the mountain "Ōmiya Mountain" and enshrining their guardian deity. While originally serving as the guardian shrine for the Tsu clan, their relocation to the [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] and the decline of the {{ill|Uji-Kabane system|ja|氏姓制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Kabane system|qq=}} led to the shrine becoming a regional guardian for nine villages. During the rise of Buddhism, the shrine adopted the name "Gozu Tennō Shrine" and incorporated Buddhist elements, establishing a Shingon sect temple, Nanno Bō, within its grounds.. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], Shinto-Buddhist syncretism was prohibited, and the temple was abolished. The shrine was renamed Ootsu Shrine in 1872 and designated as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.. Following the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the shrine became a religious corporation under the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]] in 1946.. Today, it is revered as the guardian deity for western and southwestern [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]], offering blessings for disaster prevention, safe childbirth, and household safety. == Precincts == * Main Shrine (designated [[:en:Cultural Property (Japan)|Cultural property]] of [[:en:Habikino|Habikino City]]) - Rebuilt in 1640 (Kan'ei 17)[https://www.city.habikino.lg.jp/ 羽曳野市ホームページ 大津神社]. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1640. * Heiden (offering hall) - Rebuilt in 1640. * Shrine Office. === Subsidiary Shrines === * Ōmiya Benten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵嶋姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}. Built in 1987.. * Ōmiya Inari Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}}. Built in 1972.. * Peace Shrine (Yawaragi-no-Yashiro) - Dedicated to the spirits of 278 war dead from the local community. Built in 1951 to commemorate the signing of the {{ill|San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja|サンフランシスコ講和条約|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San Francisco Peace Treaty|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本国との平和条約|1=Treaty of San Francisco|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q392541}}.. == Cultural Properties == === Habikino City Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine, with plaques. == Festivals == Two festivals are held annually, during which local roads are regulated: * Summer Festival: Held annually on July 7–8. It attracts families but has no food stalls since 2022. * Autumn Festival: Held annually on October 7–8, featuring portable shrines (mikoshi) and floats. == location == {{commons|Category:Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} * Osaka Prefecture, Habikino City, Takawashi 8-chome 1-2. == Access == * [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]] [[:en:Takawashi Station|Takawashi Station]] - About a 3-minute walk. * Nearest bus stop: {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|ja|近鉄バス松原営業所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Bus|1=Kintetsu Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639080}} Takawashi Station, 1 trip on Saturdays. * Nearest municipal bus stop: Habikino City Circulation Bus, "Usuke" Takawashi Station. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/habikino-city/m23_02_habikino_ootu.html Ootsu Shrine - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Office] {{citation needed|ja|大津神社|version=103377387|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[ja:大津神社 (羽曳野市)]] }} [[ja:大津神社]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|大津神社 (羽曳野市)|version=99966973|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Habikino, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Habikino, Osaka]] {{Commons category|Otsu-jinja (Habikino, Osaka)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōtsu Shrine (Habikino) (Q11437361)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]].
